title: (PDF) 2007 The Mystery of the Bayon Face Towers - Academia.edu
highlights:
essay examines the cultural and religious shifts in the Bàyon temple complex during and after the reign of Jayavarma Parameśvara, focusing on the iconography of the face towers. It questions the interpretations of the faces as Buddhist or Shaiva and discusses evidence of religious tolerance and the alterations made post-Jayavarman VII. The findings suggest a complex interplay between Buddhism and Hinduism through the periods, highlighting the challenges of  ... title: Statue Fragments Found Near Cambodia's Bayon Temple
highlights: reports that large statue fragments have been recovered from a canal near the Gate of the Dead at Angkor Thom by members of Cambodia’s Department of Monuments and Preventive Archaeology, the heritage police, and agents from the Apsara Authority. “The god statue found by the working team has four pieces, while another giant statue has only the back part without a face,” said Chhouk Somala of the Department of Monuments and Preventive Archaeology. The pieces are thought to have come from a sculpture depicting the Hindu story of the Churning of the Milk Ocean, in which the semi-divine half-human, half-serpent who lived on Shiva’s neck was used as a churning rope wrapped around Mount Mandara. The gods held one end of the naga, while demons held the other. As each  ...    valuables. The carvings will be conserved and housed in the Preah Norodom Sihanouk-Angkor Museum. To read about a bodhisattva sculpture unearthed at Angkor Wat's Ta Nei Temple, go to " Around the World: Cambodia." ##

title: The Bayon: A temple with many faces - Smarthistory
highlights:
Bayon: A temple with many faces byDr. Melody Rod-ari
Artwork DetailsDownward pointing arrow Bayon Temple, Angkor Thom (photo: Jakub Hałun, CC BY-SA 4.0) Bayon Temple, Angkor Thom (photo: Jakub Hałun, CC BY-SA 4.0)  Tower Faces, Bayon Temple, Angkor Thom, Cambodia (photo: Photo Dharma, CC BY 2.0) Left: Tower Faces, Bayon Temple, Angkor Thom, Cambodia; right: detail of the tower face in the red square (photo: Photo  ...    The mystery over the temple’s name contributes to the enigmatic face-towers that define the temple’s architecture. Since the Bayon’s rediscovery by French scholars and explorers in the 19th century, the identity of the smiling figures that greet visitors to the temple and to the city of Angkor Thom has been debated. Do they depict Buddhist or Hindu deities, or do they depict King Jayavarman VII smiling over his empire? Inscriptions from the temple along with its  ...
